videos.title: What is Symmetry? | Line of Symmetry for Kids
videos.description: Learn what symmetry means and how to find lines of symmetry in shapes and objects around you.
learningObjectives: Find the line of symmetry in a shape
keyConceptsCovered: A line of symmetry divides a shape into matching halves
Many objects around us have symmetry

videos.title: Symmetry Song | Fun Symmetry for Kids
videos.description: Sing along and learn about symmetry with real-life examples.
followUpTopics: Finding lines of symmetry in shapes

videos.title: Equal Halves and Parts | Folding Shapes in Half
videos.description: Learn what it means to divide a shape into two equal halves. When both halves match perfectly, that is symmetry!
learningObjectives: Fold shapes to find lines of symmetry
Verify symmetry by checking both halves match
Connect equal halves to line of symmetry
keyConceptsCovered: Folding shows the line of symmetry
Both halves are mirror images of each other
Not every line through a shape is a line of symmetry

videos.title: Basic Shapes Review | Identifying Shapes for Kids
videos.description: Review basic shapes like squares, rectangles, triangles, and circles. Knowing your shapes helps you find symmetry!
learningObjectives: Identify shapes that have lines of symmetry

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Quick introduction and reinforcement for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of line of symmetry with folding practice

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Shape review first, then line of symmetry concepts

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites
after_prerequisite_review.rationale: Essential line of symmetry introduction only

questions.question: A butterfly has a line of symmetry down the middle. What does this mean?
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry means both sides match. So both wings of the butterfly look the same!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Start with the introduction video and let students fold paper shapes to discover lines of symmetry. Use the song to make the concept memorable.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child find symmetrical objects at home like doors, windows, and plates. Fold paper shapes together to see the matching halves.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Symmetry is all around you! Look at your face in the mirror. Both sides look almost the same. That is symmetry!
